body:
PATCHPLAN for trinity_watcher.py

The existing Codex chat-window heartbeat (trinity-claude-signal-watch, 15-min cadence) is chat-window polling — not bounded, not ack-based, not autonomous. Replace it with a repo-local script.

REQUIRED BEHAVIOR:
1. --once (default): route all outbox packets via trinity_bridge.py --once logic, exit 0.
2. --probe: run trinity_windows_signal_probe.py subprocess first, include signals in output.
3. Print structured JSON: schema=digivichi.trinity.watcher-result.v0, pass, routed count, probe_signals, privacy booleans, exit=clean.
4. No while-True. No time.sleep. No persistent daemon.
5. Ack = file-move to processed/. No reprocessing (P2).
6. Raw notification text never read (P3).
7. All packets moved before exit (P4).

CONSTRAINTS:
- stdlib + project utils only; no new required deps.
- Use subprocess to call trinity_bridge.py --once rather than importing it directly.
- Write test_trinity_watcher.py (P1-P4 coverage).
- py_compile before routing the review packet.

ROUTE: outbox/antigravity -> Claude inbox, kind=review.
DO NOT land without Claude PASS verdict.

STOP GATES: no service start, no scheduled-task install, no raw notification read, no credential/config mutation, no NATS/socket.
constraints: one-shot only (P1: exit after single pass)
stdlib only; no new required deps
route to Claude for review before landing
objective: Build trinity_watcher.py: bounded one-shot listener replacing chat-window polling
requested_output: trinity_watcher.py + test_trinity_watcher.py routed to Claude inbox for review
summary: Operational order received (2026-06-14): Antigravity patches repo-local listener; Claude reviews; Codex re-enters only after 4 proofs (one pass exits, no reprocessing, no raw notification bodies, bridge packets acked). Spec: create scripts/trinity_watcher.py (one-shot, stdlib only, no while-True, subprocess-out to bridge --once + probe, exits 0, JSON summary). Test: test_trinity_watcher.py covering P1-P4. Route to Claude inbox for review before landing. Full spec: _PROJECT_KNOWLEDGE_BASE/reports/CLAUDE_LISTENER_SAFETY_BOUNDARY_REVIEW_2026-06-14.md
